content.full_text:
COLLEEN CONWAY MCANDREWS
JERRY MARGARET SIMMONS
SIMMONS & McANDREWS
ATTORNEYS ANO COUNSELORS AT LAW
IgI 
FOURTH STREET
SANTA 
MONICA, 
CALIFOR'NIA 
9O4O1
{213) 458-r405
FAX (2r3) 394-4028
/,llle il'
F pp
9ri tril ,90
August 3 | 1990
Ms. Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Dear Ms. Cassady:
This letter is to confirm the telephone advice which you
provided to me on August L, 1990 concerning the sale of goods
between candidate controlled committees.
During our telephone conversation I posed the followj-ng
scenario. A candidate controlled committee purchased a block of
tickets to an entertainment event. These tickets were to be used
in connection with a future fundraising event for the candidate.
After the tickets were purchased it was determined that the
controlled committee would not hold the fundraiser. Thus, the
conmittee desires to se1I the block of ti-ckets.
The committee was approached by a representative of another
candidate controlled committee. That committee would like to
purchase the tickets at fair market value. As I mentioned,
unlike other situations recently considered by the Commission
staff, there is no joint fundraising event or joint political
appearance associated with this proposed transaction. 
one
candidate controlled comrnittee simply wishes to dispose of
tickets which are no longer needed by the carnpaign.
After considering this issue, You verbally advised me that
it was permissible for one candidate controlled committee to se11
goods to another candidate controlled committee provided that the
goods are sold at their fair market value.


us. Lynda cassady
August 3, 1990
Page 2
ff this letter accurately summarizes your verbal advj-ce,
please sign the copy of this letter which is enclosed and return
it to me in the envelope provided. I greatly appreciate your
ongoing assistance.
JMs/dn
Enclosures
This letter accurately summarizes the verbal advice provided.
Date:
Lynda cassady

sections.question: Whether one candidate controlled committee may sell a block of tickets to an entertainment event to another candidate controlled committee at fair market value, where the tickets are no longer needed for a fundraiser and there is no joint fundraising event or joint political appearance associated with the transaction.
sections.conclusion: After considering this issue, You verbally advised me that it was permissible for one candidate controlled committee to sell goods to another candidate controlled committee provided that the goods are sold at their fair market value.
sections.question_synthetic: Can one candidate-controlled committee sell tickets to another candidate-controlled committee at fair market value when the tickets are no longer needed for the original campaign's planned fundraiser?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that one candidate-controlled committee may sell goods to another candidate-controlled committee provided the goods are sold at fair market value.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmation letter from requestor to FPPC formalizing prior verbal advice. FPPC response confirmed via signature on enclosed copy. No formal Q/C section headers present, but question and conclusion are clearly articulated in letter body. Dated August 3, 1990.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether one candidate controlled committee may sell a block of tickets to an entertainment event to another candidate controlled committee at fair market value, where the tickets are no longer needed for a fundraiser and there is no joint fundraising event or joint political appearance associated with the transaction.

CONCLUSION: After considering this issue, You verbally advised me that it was permissible for one candidate controlled committee to sell goods to another candidate controlled committee provided that the goods are sold at their fair market value.

embedding.summary: This is a confirmation letter from an attorney to FPPC Political Reform Consultant Lynda Cassady documenting verbal advice regarding the permissibility of selling tickets between candidate-controlled committees at fair market value when no joint fundraising event is involved.
